Use when you need to change a meeting time politely.
english: Could we reschedule our meeting to tomorrow?
korean: 회의를 내일로 다시 잡을 수 있을까요?

situation: Useful when explaining current progress.
english: I am working on the final draft right now.

situation: Use when giving positive feedback about a guide or process.
english: The instructions were clear and easy to follow.

situation: Useful for promising a follow-up action.
english: I will send you the file after lunch.

situation: Useful when staying in touch about plans.
english: Let me know if anything changes.

situation: Useful when you need concentration.
english: I need a quiet place to focus.
korean: 집중할 수 있는 조용한 곳이 필요해요.

situation: Use when asking for help with a specific section.
english: Could you help me with this part?
korean: 이 부분 좀 도와주실 수 있나요?

situation: Use when commenting on nice weather.
english: The weather looks perfect for a walk.

situation: Useful when talking about a small everyday problem.
english: I keep forgetting where I put my keys.

situation: Use when describing a place after a visit.
english: The cafe was much quieter than I expected.

situation: Use when politely declining plans.
english: I would rather stay home tonight.
korean: 오늘 밤에는 집에 있는 편이 더 좋아요.

situation: Use when emphasizing accuracy before sharing something.
english: We should double-check the details before sending it.
korean: 보내기 전에 세부 사항을 다시 확인해야 해요.

situation: Useful when you want to clarify a question before answering.
english: I am not sure if I understood the question correctly.

situation: Use when describing a useful tool or product.
english: This app makes it easier to build a learning habit.
korean: 이 앱은 학습 습관을 만들기 쉽게 해줘요.

situation: Useful when describing a study routine.
english: I have been reviewing these sentences every morning.
korean: 이 문장들을 매일 아침 복습하고 있어요.

situation: Useful for giving nearby directions.
english: The train station is just around the corner.

situation: Use when explaining a travel decision.
english: I booked the hotel because the location was convenient.

situation: Useful when giving an important reminder.
english: Please remember to back up your work regularly.
korean: 작업은 정기적으로 백업하는 것을 잊지 마세요.

situation: Use when reacting to a helpful explanation.
english: That explanation made the whole topic much simpler.
korean: 그 설명 덕분에 주제가 훨씬 쉬워졌어요.

situation: Useful when describing a new learning experiment.
english: I will try the new routine for a week and see how it goes.
korean: 새 루틴을 일주일 동안 해 보고 어떻게 되는지 볼게요.
